preamble: The voiceless retroflex stop [ʈ] transforms into a voiced alveolar fricative [z] through a process of assibilation and voicing. This shift is typical in environments where stops weaken into fricatives while losing their retroflexion.

examples.note: Dialectal Mandarin variation or historical shifts in certain Northern accents where retroflex stops assibilated.
Occasional assibilation of retroflex stops in specific phonetic contexts in Indic dialects.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
